subjects.name: Engineering Physics/Chemistry

course_outcomes: CO1: Comprehend the basic principle of diffraction and observe the diffraction pattern in various cases
CO2: Illustrate the concepts of Interference and polarisation and their applications
CO3: Understand the space lattices and crystal symmetry
CO4: Summarize the fundamental concepts of quantum mechanics
CO5: Explore the properties and applications of dielectric and magnetic materials
CO6: Demonstrate the conduction process of charge carriers in semiconductors
CO1: Illustrate the Molecular Orbital diagrams for simple diatomic and polyatomic molecules
CO2: Identify the applications of super conductors, semi conductors and super capacitors & nano materials
CO3: Explain the working of spectroscopy and Chromatography
CO4: Explain the properties and applications of polymers & fibres
CO5: Estimate the analytes using potentiometric & conductometric titrations and electrochemical sensors
CO6: Describe the construction and working of electrochemical cells
CO1: Comprehend the basic principle of diffraction and observe the diffraction pattern in various cases
CO2: Illustrate the concepts of interference and polarisation and their applications
CO3: Understand the space lattices and crystal symmetry
CO4: Summarize the fundamental concepts of quantum mechanics
CO5: Explore the properties and applications of dielectric and magnetic materials
CO6: Demonstrate the conduction process of charge carriers in semiconductors

subjects.name: Linear Algebra & Calculus/Differential Equations and Vector calculus

course_outcomes: CO1: Solve the system of linear equations
CO2: Construct the eigenvectors of a matrix, use the applications of Cayley Hamilton theorem
CO3: Identify the nature of the quadratic form using matrix theory
CO4: Apply Mean value theorems to solve single variable problems
CO5: Make use of partial derivatives to solve multivariable problems
CO6: Utilize multiple integrals to find the area and volume of solids
CO1: Model and solve the first order differential equations
CO2: Experiment with the practical importance of solving first and higher order differential equations in engineering
CO3: Solve higher order differential equations and understand about initial value problems
CO4: Identify methods for solving linear partial differential equations
CO5: Make use of central concepts in partial differential equations and vector differentiation
CO6: Apply vector calculus in the context of estimating the work done, circulation, flux and vector integral theorems

subjects.name: Basic Electrical and Electronics/Basic Civil & Mechanical Engineering

course_outcomes: CO1: Demonstrate the electrical parameters of DC & AC circuits
CO2: Illustrate the operation of electrical machines and power plants
CO3: Summarize electrical measurements and safety measures
CO4: Explain the operation of semiconductor devices and their characteristics
CO5: Illustrate the operation of rectifiers
CO6: Illustrate the number systems, binary codes, and combinational circuits using logic gates
CO1: Describe the basics of civil engineering streams, water resources and environmental engineering
CO2: Outline the concepts of surveying
CO3: Demonstrate the pavements and the concept of Airport, Harbour, Tunnel & Railway Engineering
CO4: Understand the different Engineering Materials, Manufacturing process and the role of mechanical Engineering in different sectors
CO5: Understand the basic concepts of thermal engineering and working principles of different power plants
CO6: Describe the working of different mechanical power transmission systems and basic configurations and applications of robots

subjects.name: Introduction to Programming
course_outcomes: CO1: Illustrate algorithms for programming and problem solving
CO2: Formulate sequential and iterative programming
CO3: Implement basic dynamic data structures
CO4: Develop modular program aspects for complex problems
CO5: Apply concepts for optimal utilization of memory
CO6: Understand techniques of storage and processing data
CO1: Understand the principles of engineering drawing, including engineering curves, scales
CO2: Draw and interpret orthographic projections of points, lines, planes in front, top and side views
CO3: Construct Orthographic projections of planes inclined to both reference planes and Projections of Solids in simple positions
CO4: Understand and apply concepts of projections of solids inclined to a reference plane and sectional views of solids in simple positions using CAD software
CO5: Gain a clear understanding of the principles behind development of surfaces and to understand how to unfold basic geometric shapes into flat patterns using CAD software
CO6: Develop the ability to draw isometric views and orthographic views and convert isometric views to orthographic views and vice versa using CAD software
CO1: Understand the context, topic, and specific information from social or transactional dialogues
CO2: Speak clearly using discourse markers on a specific topic in formal as well as informal discussions
CO3: Comprehend and appreciate literary texts by reading
CO4: Write summaries, coherent paragraphs, essays, letters/e-mails and resume
CO5: Apply grammatical structures to formulate sentence and use them appropriately
CO6: Improve communicative competence with enhanced vocabulary in formal and informal contexts
CO1: Infer the knowledge from the scientific methods and learn the process of measuring different physical parameters
CO2: Develop the laboratory skills in handling of electrical and Optical instruments
CO3: Demonstrate the interference and diffraction phenomena of light
CO4: Inspect and experience physical principles of Magnetic fields and optical fiber communications
CO5: Apply the principles of physics and measure the solid state properties of materials
CO6: Design and analyze experiment based on physics concepts
CO1: Determine the concentration of acids by Conductometry
CO2: Prepare the Bakelite polymer and ZnO nanomaterial
CO3: Estimate the strength of an acid present in secondary battery by pH metry
CO4: Identify the organic compounds by IR Spectroscopy
CO5: Verify the Beer-Lamberts law and measure the wavelength
CO6: Estimate concentration of ferrous iron by potentiometry and dichrometry
CO1: Apply fundamental electrical principles to resolve complex circuit problems
CO2: Develop proficiency in conducting electrical measurements and studying machine characteristics for practical applications
CO3: Demonstrate skills in measuring power parameters and calculate energy consumption for residential applications
CO4: Analyze the characteristics of semiconductor devices
CO5: Find the frequency response of amplifiers using BJT
CO6: Verify the truth tables of logic gates and basic flip flops
CO1: Demonstrate the simple wooden components like Cross Lap joint, T-Lap joint, Dovetail Joint
CO2: Develop the simple house hold items like Taper Tray, Square box, Open Scoop using sheet metal
CO3: Build the V-Fit, Square fit, Dovetail fit using mild steel
CO4: Understand simple house wiring circuits like Parallel/Series connection of three bulbs, Stair Case Wiring, Godown Wiring
CO5: Create the Green Sand Mould for given the patterns like rectangular shape, circular shape
CO6: Construct the metal joint using Arc Welding like: Lap joint, Butt joint

subjects.name: Computer Programming Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Read, understand and trace program execution
CO2: Select proper control structures
CO3: Construct programs using homogeneous data types
CO4: Develop modular programs using functions
CO5: Formulate memory-efficient programs
CO6: Develop programs using files

subjects.name: ECA (Yoga/Sports)
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the importance of yoga and sports for Physical fitness and sound health
CO2: Demonstrate an understanding of health-related fitness components
CO3: Compare and contrast various activities that help enhance their health
CO4: Assess current personal fitness levels
CO5: Develop Positive Personality

subjects.name: CCA (NSS/NCC/Community Service)
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the importance of discipline, character and service motto
CO2: Solve some societal issues by applying acquired knowledge, facts, and techniques
CO3: Explore human relationships by analyzing social problems
CO4: Determine to extend their help for the fellow beings and downtrodden people
CO5: Develop leadership skills and civic responsibilities

subjects.name: IT Workshop
course_outcomes: CO1: Demonstrate assembling and disassembling PC
CO2: Illustrate OS installation steps
CO3: Configure IP address and perform safe downloads
CO4: Create projects using Word/LaTeX and spreadsheets
CO5: Design PowerPoint presentations
CO6: Improve conversational abilities using AI tools

subjects.name: Communicative English Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Acquire knowledge of English sound system and pronunciation
CO2: Apply accent and intonation patterns
CO3: Write emails and resume effectively
CO4: Exhibit professionalism in debates and discussions
CO5: Demonstrate verbal and non-verbal communication
CO6: Practice interpersonal communication in formal situations

subjects.name: Data Structures (CSE, CSE-AI&DS, CSE-AI&ML, IT)
course_outcomes: CO1: Explain the role of linear data structures in organizing and accessing data efficiently in algorithms
CO2: Design, implement, and apply linked lists for dynamic data storage, demonstrating understanding of memory allocation
CO3: Develop programs using stacks to handle recursive algorithms, manage program states, and solve related problems

CO4: Apply queue-based algorithms for efficient task scheduling and breadth-first traversal in graphs and distinguish between deques and priority queues, and apply them appropriately to solve data management challenges
CO5: Devise novel solutions to small scale programming challenges involving data structures such as stacks, queues, Trees
CO6: Recognize scenarios where hashing is advantageous, and design hash-based solutions for specific problems

subjects.name: Engineering Mathematics - I
course_outcomes: CO1: Solve the system of linear equations
CO2: Construct eigenvectors of a matrix and use applications of Cayley Hamilton theorem
CO3: Identify the nature of quadratic form using matrix theory
CO4: Apply Mean value theorems to solve single variable problems
CO5: Make use of partial derivatives to solve multivariable problems
CO6: Utilize multiple integrals to find area and volume of solids

subjects.name: Engineering Physics
course_outcomes: CO1: Comprehend the basic principle of diffraction and observe the diffraction pattern in various cases
CO2: Illustrate the concepts of interference and polarisation and their applications
CO3: Understand the space lattices and crystal symmetry
CO4: Summarize the fundamental concepts of quantum mechanics
CO5: Explore the properties and applications of dielectric and magnetic materials
CO6: Demonstrate the conduction process of charge carriers in semiconductors

subjects.name: Engineering Chemistry
course_outcomes: CO1: Assess the quality of water and its treatment methods
CO2: Estimate the potentials for electrochemical cells
CO3: Describe corrosion factors and implement prevention methods
CO4: Illustrate the types of polymers and fuels with applications
CO5: Describe the concepts of colloids, micelle and nanomaterials
CO6: Explain the uses of refractory materials, lubricants and cements

subjects.name: Programming for Problem Solving (C)
course_outcomes: CO1: Illustrate algorithms for programming and problem solving
CO2: Formulate sequential and iterative programming
CO3: Implement basic dynamic data structures
CO4: Develop modular program aspects for complex problems
CO5: Apply concepts for optimal utilization of memory
CO6: Understand techniques of storage and processing data

course_outcomes: CO1: Understand principles of engineering drawing including curves and scales
CO2: Draw and interpret orthographic projections of points, lines and planes
CO3: Construct projections of planes and solids in simple positions
CO4: Apply sectional views and projections using CAD software
CO5: Understand development of surfaces using CAD
CO6: Draw and convert isometric and orthographic views using CAD

course_outcomes: CO1: Understand context and specific information from dialogues

subjects.name: Engineering Physics Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Infer knowledge from scientific methods and measure physical parameters
CO2: Develop laboratory skills in handling electrical and optical instruments
CO3: Demonstrate interference and diffraction phenomena
CO4: Inspect magnetic fields and optical fiber principles
CO5: Apply physics principles to measure solid state properties
CO6: Design and analyze physics experiments

subjects.name: Engineering Chemistry Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Assess percentage moisture of solid fuel
CO2: Prepare Bakelite polymer and ZnO nanomaterial
CO3: Determine acid number and viscosity of lubricating oil
CO4: Estimate iron and calcium in cement
CO5: Estimate hardness and dissolved oxygen in water
CO6: Determine strength of acid in lead acid battery

subjects.name: Programming Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Read, understand and trace program execution
CO2: Select proper control structures
CO3: Construct programs using homogeneous data types
CO4: Develop modular programs using functions
CO5: Formulate memory-efficient programs
CO6: Develop programs using files

subjects.name: Engineering Mathematics - II
course_outcomes: CO1: Model and solve first order differential equations
CO2: Apply first and higher order differential equations in engineering
CO3: Solve higher order differential equations and initial value problems
CO4: Identify methods for solving linear partial differential equations
CO5: Use concepts in partial differential equations and vector differentiation
CO6: Apply vector calculus for work, circulation, flux and integral theorems

subjects.name: Engineering Mechanics
course_outcomes: CO1: Compute the resultant force in concurrent and coplanar force systems
CO2: Analyze equilibrium condition for different force systems with and without friction
CO3: Determine the forces and their nature in members of trusses
CO4: Determine centroids, centre of gravity and moment of inertia of geometrical shapes
CO5: Analyze kinematics of rectilinear and curvilinear motions
CO6: Assess kinetics of rectilinear, curvilinear and rigid body motion

subjects.name: Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering
course_outcomes: CO1: Demonstrate electrical parameters of DC & AC circuits
CO2: Illustrate operation of electrical machines and power plants
CO3: Summarize electrical measurements and safety measures
CO4: Explain operation of semiconductor devices and their characteristics
CO5: Illustrate operation of rectifiers
CO6: Illustrate number systems, binary codes and combinational circuits using logic gates

subjects.name: Network Analysis
course_outcomes: CO1: Apply network reduction techniques to DC circuits
CO2: Apply network theorems to simplify complex networks
CO3: Summarize behaviour of AC circuits
CO4: Explain series and parallel connected coupled circuits
CO5: Interpret parameters of two-port networks
CO6: Illustrate transient response of R, L, C circuits for DC and AC excitations

subjects.name: Basic Electrical Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Apply electrical principles to resolve circuit problems
CO2: Develop proficiency in electrical measurements
CO3: Measure power parameters and calculate energy consumption
CO4: Analyze characteristics of semiconductor devices
CO5: Find frequency response of amplifiers using BJT
CO6: Verify truth tables of logic gates and flip flops

subjects.name: Electronics Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Apply electrical principles to resolve circuit problems
CO2: Develop proficiency in electrical measurements
CO3: Measure power parameters and calculate energy consumption
CO4: Analyze characteristics of semiconductor devices
CO5: Find frequency response of amplifiers using BJT

CO4: Create projects using Word/LaTeX and spreadsheets

course_outcomes: CO1: Model and solve first order differential equations

subjects.name: Engineering Mechanics
course_outcomes: CO1: Compute the resultant force in concurrent and coplanar force systems
CO2: Analyze equilibrium condition for different force systems with and without friction

CO5: Analyze kinematics of rectilinear and curvilinear motions

first_year.description: Universal 1st Year subjects for all branches

subjects.name: Engineering Physics
course_outcomes: CO1: Comprehend the basic principle of diffraction and observe the diffraction pattern in various cases
CO2: Illustrate the concepts of interference and polarisation and their applications
CO3: Understand the space lattices and crystal symmetry
CO4: Summarize the fundamental concepts of quantum mechanics
CO5: Explore the properties and applications of dielectric and magnetic materials
CO6: Demonstrate the conduction process of charge carriers in semiconductors

subjects.name: Chemistry
subjects.note: EEE, ECE, CSE, IT, AI&ML, AI&DS
course_outcomes: CO1: Illustrate the Molecular Orbital diagrams for simple diatomic and polyatomic molecules
CO2: Identify the applications of superconductors, semiconductors, super capacitors & nano materials
CO3: Explain the working of spectroscopy and chromatography
CO4: Explain the properties and applications of polymers & fibres
CO5: Estimate analytes using potentiometric & conductometric titrations and electrochemical sensors
CO6: Describe the construction and working of electrochemical cells

subjects.name: Engineering Chemistry
course_outcomes: CO1: Assess the quality of water and its treatment methods
CO2: Estimate the potentials for electrochemical cells
CO3: Describe corrosion factors and implement prevention methods
CO4: Illustrate the types of polymers and fuels with applications
CO5: Describe the concepts of colloids, micelle and nanomaterials
CO6: Explain the uses of refractory materials, lubricants and cements

subjects.name: Linear Algebra & Calculus
course_outcomes: CO1: Solve the system of linear equations
CO2: Construct eigenvectors of a matrix and use applications of Cayley Hamilton theorem
CO3: Identify the nature of quadratic form using matrix theory
CO4: Apply Mean value theorems to solve single variable problems
CO5: Make use of partial derivatives to solve multivariable problems
CO6: Utilize multiple integrals to find area and volume of solids

subjects.name: Differential Equations & Vector Calculus
course_outcomes: CO1: Model and solve first order differential equations
CO2: Apply first and higher order differential equations in engineering
CO3: Solve higher order differential equations and initial value problems
CO4: Identify methods for solving linear partial differential equations
CO5: Use concepts in partial differential equations and vector differentiation
CO6: Apply vector calculus for work, circulation, flux and integral theorems

subjects.name: Basic Electrical & Electronics Engineering
course_outcomes: CO1: Demonstrate electrical parameters of DC & AC circuits
CO2: Illustrate operation of electrical machines and power plants
CO3: Summarize electrical measurements and safety measures
CO4: Explain operation of semiconductor devices and their characteristics
CO5: Illustrate operation of rectifiers
CO6: Illustrate number systems, binary codes and combinational circuits using logic gates

subjects.name: Basic Civil & Mechanical Engineering
course_outcomes: CO1: Describe basics of civil engineering streams, water resources and environmental engineering
CO2: Outline concepts of surveying
CO3: Demonstrate pavements and concept of Airport, Harbour, Tunnel & Railway Engineering
CO4: Understand engineering materials, manufacturing process and role of mechanical engineering
CO5: Understand basic concepts of thermal engineering and working principles of power plants
CO6: Describe working of mechanical power transmission systems and configurations of robots

subjects.name: Introduction to Programming
course_outcomes: CO1: Illustrate algorithms for programming and problem solving
CO2: Formulate sequential and iterative programming
CO3: Implement basic dynamic data structures
CO4: Develop modular program aspects for complex problems
CO5: Apply concepts for optimal utilization of memory
CO6: Understand techniques of storage and processing data

subjects.name: Engineering Graphics
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand principles of engineering drawing including curves and scales
CO2: Draw and interpret orthographic projections of points, lines and planes
CO3: Construct projections of planes and solids in simple positions
CO4: Apply sectional views and projections using CAD software
